channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Beyond Wind: Fully Funded PhD Opportunity in Tropical Cyclone Risk, Flooding, and Landslides at Loughborough University
item.dc:creator: Dennis Mwangi
item.description:
Climate-related disasters are becoming more frequent and devastating across the world, with tropical cyclones continuing to threaten millions of lives, infrastructure, economies, and ecosystems every year. While many studies focus primarily on the destructive wind speeds associated with tropical cyclones, researchers are increasingly discovering that the most severe losses often occur when multiple hazards happen […]

The post Beyond Wind: Fully Funded PhD Opportunity in Tropical Cyclone Risk, Flooding, and Landslides at Loughborough University appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Climate-related disasters are becoming more frequent and devastating across the world, with tropical cyclones continuing to threaten millions of lives, infrastructure, economies, and ecosystems every year. While many studies focus primarily on the destructive wind speeds associated with tropical cyclones, researchers are increasingly discovering that the most severe losses often occur when multiple hazards happen at the same time — especially flooding and landslides.

To address this critical global challenge, Loughborough University is offering an exciting fully funded PhD project titled “Beyond Wind! Where and Why Flooding and Landslides Amplify Tropical Cyclone Risk (Ref: IRISK-26-LU-03)” under its Department of Geography.

This prestigious doctoral opportunity is part of the Informatics for Multi-hazard Risk and Resilience (i-Risk) NERC Doctoral Focal Awards (DFA) and is designed for students who are passionate about climate science, disaster resilience, artificial intelligence, environmental hazards, and risk analysis.

The project combines cutting-edge AI technologies, satellite observations, hazard modelling, and disaster risk reduction strategies to improve understanding of how multiple hazards interact during tropical cyclones and how these combined hazards increase losses globally.

About the PhD Project

Tropical cyclones are among the world’s most destructive natural disasters. Although strong winds are often considered the primary threat, tropical cyclones also trigger:

  • Severe flooding
  • Coastal inundation
  • Landslides
  • Infrastructure collapse
  • Economic losses
  • Human displacement

However, scientists still lack a comprehensive understanding of how these hazards occur together and how they amplify overall disaster risks.

This PhD project seeks to fill that important knowledge gap by creating the first global and systematic record of tropical cyclone hazards involving:

  1. Wind hazards
  2. Flooding events
  3. Landslide occurrences

Using advanced satellite data and world-leading artificial intelligence tools, the research will investigate:

  • Where multiple hazards co-occur
  • Why they happen simultaneously
  • How climate patterns influence compound hazards
  • Why combined hazards produce greater destruction
  • How disaster forecasting systems can improve early warning capabilities

The project will also use detailed case studies to analyze how multiple hazards amplify losses during major tropical cyclone events.

The findings are expected to provide critical insights for:

  • Insurance industries
  • Climate adaptation planners
  • Disaster risk reduction agencies
  • Emergency response systems
  • Early warning systems
  • Infrastructure resilience planning

Research Placements and International Exposure

One of the major highlights of this PhD opportunity is the planned research placements at globally respected institutions, including:

  • British Geological Survey
  • National Center for Atmospheric Research

These placements will provide students with:

  • International research exposure
  • Access to advanced scientific tools
  • Collaboration opportunities with leading experts
  • Practical experience in hazard and climate research
  • Professional networking opportunities

Supervisory Team

The project will be supervised by experienced researchers in geography, climate science, and disaster resilience.

Primary Supervisor

  • Dr. John Hillier

Secondary Supervisor

  • Prof. Dilanthi Amaratunga

Applicants are encouraged to contact potential supervisors before submitting their applications to discuss the project and research interests.

Funding Benefits

This PhD studentship is funded by UKRI and provides extensive financial support for successful candidates.

The funding package includes:

  • Tax-free annual stipend of £21,805
  • Tuition fees covered at the UK rate
  • Duration of funding: 3.5 years
  • Potential full international fee waiver for outstanding international students
  • Research and professional development opportunities
  • International placements and collaborations

This makes the programme highly competitive and financially attractive for both UK and international applicants.

Study Duration and Mode

Applicants can choose between:

Full-Time Option

  • Duration: 3.5 years

Part-Time Option

  • Duration: 7 years

Start Date

  • October 2026

Eligibility Requirements

To qualify for this PhD opportunity, applicants must possess:

  • A strong undergraduate degree or Master’s degree
  • At least a UK 2:1 honours degree or equivalent international qualification
  • Academic background in a relevant discipline

Relevant academic fields may include:

  • Civil Engineering
  • Climate Science
  • Environmental Science
  • Geography
  • Data Science
  • Hazard Modelling
  • Earth Sciences
  • Disaster Risk Management
  • AI and Informatics

International applicants must also satisfy English language requirements.

English Language Requirements

Applicants whose first language is not English are expected to demonstrate English proficiency through recognized tests such as:

  • IELTS
  • TOEFL

Minimum IELTS requirement:

  • Overall score: 6.5
  • At least 6.0 in each competency

Application Process

Applications must be submitted online through Loughborough University.

Applicants should:

  1. Select School of Social Science and Humanities under programme name
  2. Quote the project reference:
    • IRISK-26-LU-03

Required Application Documents

Candidates must upload the following documents:

1. Personal Statement (Two Pages)

The statement should include:

Section One

Research interests in:

  • Informatics
  • Disaster risk reduction
  • i-Risk DFA
  • Motivation for selecting the project
Section Two

Responses to competency questions covering:

  • Problem-solving
  • Data analytics experience
  • Goal achievement
  • Handling setbacks and troubleshooting

2. Curriculum Vitae (CV)

The CV should include:

  • Academic background
  • Research interests
  • Relevant experience

3. Academic Transcripts and Certificates

Documents must be translated into English if necessary.

4. IELTS or TOEFL Certificate

Required for applicable international applicants.

Selection Criteria

Applications will be assessed based on:

  • Motivation and career aspirations
  • Intellectual excellence and academic potential
  • Suitability for the project
  • Alignment with i-Risk objectives

Why This PhD Opportunity Matters

This project addresses one of the world’s most urgent climate and disaster challenges.

As climate change intensifies extreme weather events, understanding compound hazards becomes increasingly important for protecting communities and infrastructure.

The successful candidate will contribute to:

  • Global disaster resilience research
  • Climate adaptation strategies
  • AI-driven hazard analysis
  • Improved early warning systems
  • Future climate risk policies

This is an exceptional opportunity for students interested in combining technology, climate science, and disaster resilience to create real-world impact.

Important Deadline

Application Deadline: 9 June 2026

Interested candidates are encouraged to prepare their applications early due to the competitive nature of the studentship.

CLICK TO APPLY

For more information about this opportunity, visit here.

Discover more Global PhD Scholarship Opportunities on OFY here.

The post Beyond Wind: Fully Funded PhD Opportunity in Tropical Cyclone Risk, Flooding, and Landslides at Loughborough University appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Manus Fellow Program 2026: Join a Global Network Shaping the Future of AI Agents
item.dc:creator: Dennis Mwangi
item.description:
The future of artificial intelligence is rapidly evolving, and the Manus Fellow Program 2026 is creating an opportunity for innovators, entrepreneurs, AI experts, and community builders worldwide to take part in that transformation. Designed for AI-native thinkers and industry leaders, the Manus Fellowship is a hands-on immersive program that empowers professionals to explore the frontier […]

The post Manus Fellow Program 2026: Join a Global Network Shaping the Future of AI Agents appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The future of artificial intelligence is rapidly evolving, and the Manus Fellow Program 2026 is creating an opportunity for innovators, entrepreneurs, AI experts, and community builders worldwide to take part in that transformation.

Designed for AI-native thinkers and industry leaders, the Manus Fellowship is a hands-on immersive program that empowers professionals to explore the frontier of general AI agents while helping organizations and communities adopt AI-powered workflows and automation.

From hosting professional events to mentoring business teams and demonstrating real-world AI automation impact, Manus Fellows are becoming global voices driving the next generation of intelligent systems.

If you are passionate about AI, productivity, innovation, entrepreneurship, or business transformation, this fellowship offers a unique opportunity to collaborate with a growing international network of forward-thinking professionals.

What is the Manus Fellow Program?

The Manus Fellow Program is a global fellowship initiative focused on helping professionals and innovators shape the future of general AI agents.

The program brings together:

  • AI enthusiasts
  • Industry experts
  • Startup founders
  • Builders and hackers
  • Community leaders
  • Consultants
  • Students
  • Product growth specialists
  • Automation advocates

Through the fellowship, selected fellows work closely with the Manus team to explore advanced AI-driven workflows and demonstrate how AI agents can improve productivity, operations, and business performance.

The program encourages fellows to become ambassadors of AI-powered transformation within their communities and industries.

Mission of the Manus Fellowship

The Manus Fellowship aims to:

  1. Accelerate AI adoption globally
  2. Build strong AI-focused communities
  3. Showcase real-world AI automation use cases
  4. Encourage innovation in agent-based workflows
  5. Help shape the future development of Manus products

The fellowship supports professionals who are actively experimenting with AI tools and applying them in practical business and operational environments.

Who Should Apply for the Manus Fellowship?

The Manus Fellow Program is looking for individuals who are actively contributing to the AI ecosystem and helping others understand the power of intelligent automation.

Ideal applicants include:

1. Local Community Builders and KOLs

The program seeks community organizers and key opinion leaders across:

  • Artificial intelligence
  • Entrepreneurship
  • Productivity
  • Technology
  • Creator economy
  • Innovation ecosystems

Applicants who host events, build communities, or educate audiences about AI technologies are highly encouraged to apply.

2. Industry Experts

Professionals with experience implementing AI solutions in real business settings can play an important role in the fellowship.

This includes individuals who can:

  • Demonstrate AI ROI
  • Improve business workflows
  • Automate operations
  • Lead digital transformation projects
  • Train teams on AI adoption

3. Manus Power Users

The fellowship welcomes advanced users who push AI tools to their limits and actively explore what intelligent agents can accomplish.

These applicants may already be:

  • Testing complex workflows
  • Building AI systems
  • Creating automation frameworks
  • Experimenting with AI integrations
  • Developing productivity systems

4. AI-First Innovators

The program is also ideal for innovators building AI-native systems, workflows, and organizations.

This may include:

  • Startup founders
  • Product builders
  • AI consultants
  • Operations specialists
  • Researchers
  • Workflow architects

Benefits of the Manus Fellow Program

Selected Manus Fellows receive access to multiple benefits designed to help them grow their influence, expand their AI expertise, and strengthen their communities.

Fellowship Benefits Include:

Event Support and Community Funding

Fellows receive:

  • Event stipends
  • Community initiative support
  • Official Manus swag
  • Resources for local activations

This enables fellows to organize impactful events and educational sessions within their regions.

Free Manus Credits

Participants gain free access to Manus credits throughout the fellowship duration, allowing them to:

  • Explore advanced workflows
  • Test AI automation systems
  • Build experimental use cases
  • Create scalable AI-powered processes

Direct Access to the Manus Team

Fellows receive a direct communication channel with the Manus team, providing opportunities for:

  • Product feedback
  • Collaboration
  • Networking
  • Insights into upcoming developments

Early Access to Product Releases

Selected fellows may receive early access to new Manus features and releases before they become publicly available.

Visibility and Recognition

The fellowship provides exposure through:

  • Community shoutouts
  • Global recognition
  • Featured opportunities
  • Speaking engagements
  • Event hosting opportunities

This visibility can significantly strengthen a fellow’s professional profile in the AI ecosystem.

Responsibilities of Manus Fellows

During the fellowship, fellows are expected to contribute actively to the growth and adoption of AI-powered systems.

Responsibilities may include:

  • Translating complex workflows into AI-agent solutions
  • Hosting community events and activations
  • Demonstrating AI implementation strategies
  • Sharing product feedback
  • Educating businesses and communities about AI automation
  • Building local or digital AI communities

Global Fellowship Network

The Manus Fellowship Network is rapidly expanding across major global cities and innovation hubs.

Cities and Regions Mentioned Include:

  • New York
  • San Francisco
  • Paris
  • London
  • Tokyo
  • Seoul
  • Singapore
  • Riyadh

The program also supports participation across:

  • Entrepreneurship hubs
  • Consulting ecosystems
  • Retail industries
  • Tech communities
  • Creator economies
  • Student innovation networks

Meet the Manus Fellows

The fellowship has already attracted a diverse and impressive group of innovators from around the world.

Some featured fellows include:

  • Abhishek Suryawanshi – AI and open knowledge advocate from San Francisco
  • Ahmed Ezzat – AI engineer from Cairo
  • Brandon Chen – Entrepreneur and community builder from Berkeley
  • Cho Chan Myei Oo – Product growth and partnership expert from Singapore
  • Danilo Brizola – CEO and AI enabler from Brazil
  • Eva Wedra Kačková – Founder and AI explorer from Slovakia
  • Gary Liew – Entrepreneurship and tech-focused MBA student from London
  • Glen Gao – Builder with experience at Meta and HRT
  • Grant McNaughton – Founder and entrepreneur from San Francisco

These fellows represent the diversity and global reach of the Manus community.

Why the Manus Fellow Program Matters

Artificial intelligence is changing how businesses operate, how teams collaborate, and how productivity systems are designed.

Programs like the Manus Fellowship are important because they:

  • Help democratize access to AI innovation
  • Empower local AI ecosystems
  • Encourage responsible AI experimentation
  • Promote collaboration between industries and communities
  • Accelerate practical AI adoption worldwide

As organizations increasingly seek AI-driven efficiency and automation, professionals who understand intelligent workflows will become even more valuable.

How to Apply

Interested applicants can apply directly through the Manus Fellowship application portal.

Applicants should prepare to showcase:

  • Their AI experience
  • Community involvement
  • Innovation projects
  • Business transformation initiatives
  • AI workflow experimentation
  • Leadership in AI-related spaces

Deadline

No official application deadline has been stated.

Applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible.

APPLY HERE

For more information about this opportunity, visit here.

Discover more Global Fellowship Opportunities on OFY here.

The post Manus Fellow Program 2026: Join a Global Network Shaping the Future of AI Agents appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Fully Funded PhD at Loughborough University 2026 in Multi-Hazard Early Warning Systems and Disaster Resilience (IRISK-26-LU-02)
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
Applications are now open for a fully funded PhD opportunity at Loughborough University focusing on the technical, legal, and socio-cultural complexities involved in communicating multi-hazard early warning systems to jurisdictional agencies and emergency response partners. Hosted within the School of Architecture, Building and Civil Engineering, this doctoral research project forms part of the prestigious Informatics […]

The post Fully Funded PhD at Loughborough University 2026 in Multi-Hazard Early Warning Systems and Disaster Resilience (IRISK-26-LU-02) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for a fully funded PhD opportunity at Loughborough University focusing on the technical, legal, and socio-cultural complexities involved in communicating multi-hazard early warning systems to jurisdictional agencies and emergency response partners.

Hosted within the School of Architecture, Building and Civil Engineering, this doctoral research project forms part of the prestigious Informatics for Multi-hazard Risk and Resilience (i-Risk) NERC Doctoral Focal Awards in Environmental Sciences.

The project is supervised by Prof Dilanthi Amaratunga and Dr John Hillier and offers an outstanding opportunity for researchers interested in disaster risk reduction, climate resilience, governance systems, informatics, civil engineering, and emergency management.

With global climate risks intensifying and disaster events becoming increasingly complex, the need for effective Multi-Hazard Early Warning Systems (MHEWS) has become a major international priority. This PhD project aims to investigate one of the most overlooked areas within disaster governance: the interface between hazard detection systems and operational emergency response agencies.

About the PhD Project

The research project was developed in response to the devastating coastal floods that affected South Asia in late 2025, highlighting urgent global weaknesses in disaster preparedness and emergency communication systems.

Although governance structures are widely recognized as essential to sustainable disaster management systems, the transition point between upstream hazard monitoring systems and downstream emergency response operations remains poorly understood.

This “interface” is often characterized by:

  • Jurisdictional complexity
  • Institutional fragmentation
  • Communication barriers
  • Legal coordination challenges
  • Technical interoperability issues
  • Socio-cultural response dynamics

The PhD research seeks to examine these governance arrangements in detail and identify ways to strengthen coordination between:

  • Regional information providers
  • Hazard detection systems
  • National agencies
  • Sub-national emergency operations centers
  • Disaster response stakeholders

Ultimately, the project aims to contribute toward long-term disaster resilience and improved multi-hazard preparedness frameworks globally.

Research Areas and Academic Focus

This interdisciplinary PhD combines multiple fields including:

  • Civil Engineering
  • Climate Science
  • Politics and Government
  • Disaster Risk Reduction
  • Environmental Informatics
  • Governance and Public Policy
  • Emergency Management
  • Resilience Studies

The project is particularly suitable for candidates interested in understanding how technology, governance, and human systems interact during disaster response and resilience planning.

Funding Information

This is a fully funded UKRI studentship covering 3.5 years of doctoral study.

Funding includes:

  • A tax-free stipend of £21,805 per year
  • Tuition fees at the UK rate
  • Potential full international tuition fee waiver for outstanding international applicants

The programme notes that, due to UKRI funding regulations, no more than 30% of funded studentships can be awarded to international students.

Funding Duration

  • Full-time: 3.5 years
  • Part-time: 7 years

Start Date

  • October 2026

Bench Fees

  • No bench fees required

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants must:

  • Hold, or expect to obtain shortly, a strong undergraduate or Master’s degree
  • Have at least a UK 2:1 honours degree or equivalent international qualification
  • Graduate from a recognized high-ranking university
  • Demonstrate strong academic and research potential

Relevant academic backgrounds may include:

  • Civil Engineering
  • Climate Science
  • Environmental Science
  • Disaster Risk Management
  • Geography
  • Political Science
  • Public Policy
  • Data Science
  • Informatics
  • Governance Studies
  • Related interdisciplinary fields

English Language Requirements

International applicants must meet the university’s English language requirements.

Applicants are expected to achieve:

  • IELTS score of 6.5 overall
  • Minimum 6.0 in each competency

Equivalent TOEFL or recognized English proficiency qualifications may also be accepted.

What Applicants Must Submit

During the online application process, candidates must upload:

Personal Statement

A two-page personal statement divided into two sections:

Section One

One page discussing:

  • Research interests in informatics and disaster risk reduction
  • Interest in the i-Risk Doctoral Focal Award
  • Rationale for selecting the project

Section Two

Responses to four reflective questions addressing:

  • Problem-solving and innovation
  • Data analytics experience
  • Goal-setting and motivation
  • Handling setbacks and troubleshooting

Additional Documents

Applicants must also provide:

  • Curriculum Vitae (CV)
  • Academic transcripts
  • Degree certificates
  • IELTS or TOEFL certificate (if applicable)

Application Assessment Criteria

Applications will be evaluated based on:

  • Motivation and career aspirations
  • Intellectual excellence and research potential
  • Suitability for the specific project
  • Alignment with the i-Risk programme

Interview Process

Shortlisted candidates are expected to attend remote interviews via Microsoft Teams during the week commencing 29 June 2026.

Applicants are also encouraged to contact supervisors before applying to discuss project-specific questions.

How to Apply

Applications must be submitted online through Loughborough University.

Applicants should:

  • Select “Architecture, Building and Civil Engineering” under programme name
  • Quote the reference: IRISK-26-LU-02

Important Contacts

  • General enquiries: irisk@mailbox.lboro.ac.uk
  • School contact: abce.pgr@mailbox.lboro.ac.uk

Why Study at Loughborough University?

Loughborough University is internationally recognized for excellence in teaching, research, and postgraduate education.

The university has received recognition through the FindAMasters and FindAPhD Postgrad Awards and is known for:

  • High-quality research supervision
  • Strong doctoral training environments
  • International academic reputation
  • Supportive postgraduate community
  • Interdisciplinary research opportunities

Doctoral researchers at Loughborough gain opportunities to contribute new knowledge to globally significant challenges while developing advanced academic, analytical, and professional skills.

Application Deadline

📅 Closing Date: 9 June 2026

Interested applicants are encouraged to begin preparing their materials early due to the competitive nature of UKRI-funded doctoral positions.

Final Thoughts

This fully funded PhD at Loughborough University offers a rare opportunity to conduct impactful interdisciplinary research at the intersection of disaster governance, climate resilience, informatics, and emergency response systems.

For students passionate about disaster risk reduction, policy coordination, climate resilience, and sustainable governance systems, this project provides not only financial support but also access to a world-class research environment tackling some of the most pressing global challenges of our time.

For more information click here 

Discover more opportunities here 

The post Fully Funded PhD at Loughborough University 2026 in Multi-Hazard Early Warning Systems and Disaster Resilience (IRISK-26-LU-02) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: AgDevCo Agribusiness Financing Opportunity 2026: Multi-Million-Dollar Investment Support for Agribusinesses in Sub-Saharan Africa
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
Agribusinesses across Sub-Saharan Africa now have the opportunity to access large-scale financing and growth support through the 2026 AgDevCo Agribusiness Financing Opportunity, an investment initiative designed to strengthen Africa’s agricultural sector and accelerate sustainable food systems development. Led by AgDevCo, this financing opportunity supports commercially viable agribusinesses that are ready to scale operations, expand market […]

The post AgDevCo Agribusiness Financing Opportunity 2026: Multi-Million-Dollar Investment Support for Agribusinesses in Sub-Saharan Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Agribusinesses across Sub-Saharan Africa now have the opportunity to access large-scale financing and growth support through the 2026 AgDevCo Agribusiness Financing Opportunity, an investment initiative designed to strengthen Africa’s agricultural sector and accelerate sustainable food systems development.

Led by AgDevCo, this financing opportunity supports commercially viable agribusinesses that are ready to scale operations, expand market reach, improve agricultural productivity, and create measurable development impact across African economies.

Unlike conventional grants or short-term startup funding programmes, AgDevCo provides long-term investment solutions tailored to growth-stage agribusinesses operating across the agricultural value chain.

The initiative focuses on businesses capable of driving transformation in African food systems while creating employment opportunities, supporting smallholder farmers, and contributing to environmentally sustainable agricultural development.

What Is the AgDevCo Agribusiness Financing Opportunity?

The AgDevCo Agribusiness Financing Opportunity is a major investment platform that provides strategic financing and technical support to agribusinesses operating in Sub-Saharan Africa.

The programme is specifically designed for businesses with proven operational models, strong management structures, and scalable commercial potential.

AgDevCo invests in enterprises that can generate both financial returns and long-term development impact. The organization prioritizes investments capable of strengthening food systems, improving agricultural productivity, increasing market access, and supporting inclusive economic growth across Africa.

Rather than offering one-size-fits-all funding, AgDevCo structures investments based on the needs, stage, and growth potential of each business.

Types of Financial Support Available

Selected agribusinesses may benefit from several forms of financing and growth support, including:

Long-Term Debt Financing

AgDevCo provides patient capital through long-term debt structures designed to support business expansion, infrastructure development, operational scaling, and production growth.

Equity Investment

Businesses with high growth potential may receive equity investment to support long-term strategic expansion and market development.

Working Capital Support

Agribusinesses often face liquidity and seasonal cash-flow challenges. AgDevCo offers working capital financing to help businesses maintain operational stability and growth momentum.

Technical Assistance

Beyond financing, selected businesses may also receive technical assistance aimed at strengthening operations, improving governance, enhancing ESG compliance, and supporting sustainable scaling.

This blended support model helps agribusinesses not only secure capital but also build the institutional and operational capacity required for long-term success.

Priority Investment Areas

AgDevCo welcomes applications from agribusinesses operating across multiple segments of the agricultural value chain.

Priority sectors include:

Primary Production and Farming

This includes crop farming, livestock production, aquaculture, and other forms of agricultural primary production capable of improving food security and economic inclusion.

Agro-Processing and Value Addition

Businesses involved in transforming raw agricultural products into higher-value goods are strongly encouraged to apply.

Agricultural Inputs and Distribution

Companies providing seeds, fertilizers, mechanization, irrigation systems, and agricultural technologies are eligible for consideration.

Cold Chain and Logistics

AgDevCo supports infrastructure and logistics solutions that improve storage, transportation, and post-harvest management across food systems.

Market Systems and Aggregation

Enterprises improving market access, aggregation systems, supply chain coordination, and trade efficiency may qualify for support.

Climate-Smart Agriculture

The programme prioritizes sustainable agricultural solutions capable of improving resilience, reducing environmental impact, and supporting climate adaptation.

Food Systems Infrastructure

Investments may also support infrastructure projects that strengthen food systems and agricultural market ecosystems.

Funding Size and Investment Range

One of the most significant aspects of this opportunity is the scale of financing available.

AgDevCo does not impose a fixed funding amount. Instead, investments are structured according to the needs and growth potential of each business.

Historically, AgDevCo investments have ranged from approximately US$1 million to over US$15 million.

This makes the programme particularly suitable for established agribusinesses seeking substantial growth capital rather than early-stage startup grants.

Who Can Apply?

The opportunity is open to agribusinesses operating in Sub-Saharan Africa.

Eligible applicants may include:

  • Agricultural producers
  • Agro-processing companies
  • Agritech businesses
  • Food system infrastructure companies
  • Agricultural logistics providers
  • Market aggregation enterprises
  • Climate-smart agriculture ventures
  • Agricultural supply chain businesses

AgDevCo accepts applications from businesses operating in low- and middle-income countries across Sub-Saharan Africa.

The organization may also consider investments in upper-middle-income countries such as South Africa where:

  • There is strong development impact potential
  • The business demonstrates regional expansion ambitions
  • There is intent to invest in lower-income African markets

Selection Criteria

AgDevCo evaluates each application based on merit and alignment with its investment standards.

Key selection criteria include:

Proven Business Model

Applicants should demonstrate a commercially viable and operational business model supported by a strong business plan and financial track record.

Strong Revenue Performance

Businesses should show evidence of consistent revenue generation and operational sustainability.

Growth and Scaling Potential

The programme prioritizes enterprises with clear opportunities for long-term expansion and market impact.

Experienced Management Team

A strong leadership and operational team is considered essential for successful investment partnerships.

Development Impact

AgDevCo seeks businesses capable of creating measurable positive impact, particularly for:

  • Smallholder farmers
  • Rural communities
  • Job creation
  • Food systems resilience
  • Economic inclusion

ESG and Regulatory Compliance

Applicants must comply with applicable local and international regulations and demonstrate commitment to environmental, social, and governance (ESG) best practices.

Commercial Sustainability

Businesses should demonstrate the ability to achieve sustainable long-term commercial performance.

Responsible Investment and Sustainability

Responsible investment principles are central to AgDevCo’s approach.

Potential investees are expected to:

  • Comply with local and international laws
  • Align with AgDevCo’s Responsible Business Principles
  • Work toward international ESG standards
  • Implement structured sustainability and governance improvement plans

AgDevCo ultimately measures investment success not only through financial returns but also through long-term transformational impact on communities, economies, and the environment.

Excluded Sectors

AgDevCo supports projects across most agricultural value chains; however, businesses involved in:

  • Tobacco
  • Potable ethanol

are not eligible for investment consideration.

Final Thoughts

The 2026 AgDevCo Agribusiness Financing Opportunity represents a major opportunity for growth-stage agribusinesses seeking substantial capital and strategic support to scale operations across Africa.

With investment sizes reaching multi-million-dollar levels and support extending beyond financing into technical assistance and ESG strengthening, AgDevCo offers a unique platform for businesses capable of driving sustainable transformation within African food systems.

For agribusiness leaders committed to long-term impact, commercial sustainability, and inclusive agricultural development, this opportunity could serve as a powerful catalyst for expansion and regional growth.

For more information click here 

Discover more opportunities here 

The post AgDevCo Agribusiness Financing Opportunity 2026: Multi-Million-Dollar Investment Support for Agribusinesses in Sub-Saharan Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Africa Youth Future Forward Innovation Challenge 2026: Win Funding, Meet Investors, and Scale Your Impact Across Africa
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
Young African innovators now have an exciting opportunity to showcase transformative ideas, access investors, and compete for funding through the Africa Youth Future Forward Innovation Challenge 2026, set to take place in Johannesburg, South Africa, on 8–9 October 2026. Organized by ICOYACA South Africa in partnership with Daraja Impact Hub Ltd and Africa Youth Forum […]

The post Africa Youth Future Forward Innovation Challenge 2026: Win Funding, Meet Investors, and Scale Your Impact Across Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Young African innovators now have an exciting opportunity to showcase transformative ideas, access investors, and compete for funding through the Africa Youth Future Forward Innovation Challenge 2026, set to take place in Johannesburg, South Africa, on 8–9 October 2026.

Organized by ICOYACA South Africa in partnership with Daraja Impact Hub Ltd and Africa Youth Forum International – AYFI, this continental innovation challenge is designed to empower the next generation of African changemakers who are building scalable, technology-driven, and socially impactful solutions.

The challenge creates a platform where ambitious young founders, innovators, entrepreneurs, and technology leaders can pitch solutions to real-world African challenges while gaining access to mentorship, funding opportunities, market-entry support, and investor networks.

With Africa rapidly emerging as a global hub for youth-led innovation and entrepreneurship, initiatives like this challenge are helping bridge the gap between groundbreaking ideas and the resources needed to scale them into sustainable ventures.

Why the Africa Youth Future Forward Innovation Challenge Matters

Across Africa, many promising innovations fail to grow due to limited access to capital, mentorship, strategic partnerships, and visibility. While young innovators continue to develop solutions for pressing economic, environmental, and social challenges, many struggle to connect with the ecosystems capable of helping them scale.

The Africa Youth Future Forward Innovation Challenge was created specifically to address this issue.

Rather than functioning as just another competition, the initiative is structured as an innovation pipeline that connects young African innovators with practical growth opportunities. Participants are not only evaluated on their ideas but are also supported through accelerator-style mentorship, ecosystem access, and investor engagement opportunities.

The challenge aims to amplify youth-led innovation while promoting solutions aligned with Africa’s future development priorities, including sustainability, digital transformation, trade, economic inclusion, and social impact.

What Participants Will Gain

Selected participants will benefit from a wide range of opportunities designed to support both early-stage and growth-stage innovations.

Key benefits include:

  • A structured accelerator and mentorship programme
  • Direct investor and corporate engagement opportunities
  • Continental visibility and networking opportunities
  • Access to innovation ecosystems and strategic partners
  • Live pitching opportunities during the Africa Youth Future Forward Summit in Johannesburg
  • Practical market-entry and scaling support
  • Exposure to industry leaders, ecosystem builders, and policymakers
  • A chance to win the R20,000 Grand Prize funding

The challenge is particularly valuable for innovators seeking not only financial support but also long-term ecosystem integration and strategic guidance.

Focus Areas and Innovation Tracks

Applicants are encouraged to submit innovative solutions that address major African development challenges through technology, entrepreneurship, sustainability, and social innovation.

The challenge includes five major innovation tracks:

1. AI, Data and Digital Transformation

This track focuses on digital innovation, artificial intelligence, big data, automation, and emerging technologies capable of transforming industries and public services across Africa.

Innovators developing AI-driven platforms, digital tools, smart systems, cybersecurity solutions, educational technology, or data-driven services are encouraged to apply.

2. Climate, Energy and Green Economy

Africa faces urgent environmental and climate-related challenges, creating demand for innovative green solutions.

This track welcomes innovations in renewable energy, climate adaptation, waste management, sustainable agriculture, clean technology, circular economy models, and environmental sustainability.

3. Entrepreneurship and Economic Inclusion

Economic empowerment remains central to Africa’s growth agenda.

Applicants under this track may include solutions promoting financial inclusion, SME development, youth employment, digital finance, community entrepreneurship, or inclusive economic systems.

4. Youth Wellness and Social Impact

This category focuses on innovations designed to improve social wellbeing and quality of life for African communities.

Projects related to healthcare, mental health, education, youth empowerment, gender inclusion, social services, and community development are strongly encouraged.

5. Smart Trade and AfCFTA Enablement

With the African Continental Free Trade Area (AfCFTA) reshaping trade opportunities across the continent, this track focuses on solutions that facilitate cross-border trade, logistics, market integration, trade technology, and economic connectivity.

Innovators building digital trade platforms, logistics systems, fintech trade tools, or market access solutions may find this category especially relevant.

Event Location and Timeline

The Africa Youth Future Forward Innovation Challenge will culminate during the Africa Youth Future Forward Summit in Johannesburg, South Africa, on 8–9 October 2026.

The summit will bring together innovators, investors, ecosystem leaders, corporate stakeholders, and development actors from across the continent.

Participants selected for live pitching will have the opportunity to showcase their innovations before a diverse audience capable of supporting scaling and implementation.

Application Requirements

Applicants are expected to prepare the following materials before submission:

  • A PDF pitch deck
  • A 3-minute video pitch
  • Core implementation milestones for the next 12 months

The application process is divided into five major sections:

  1. Applicant Information
  2. Team Information
  3. Innovation Details
  4. Implementation and Support Needs
  5. Declaration and Final Submission

Applicants should ensure their submissions clearly explain:

  • The problem being addressed
  • The proposed solution
  • The technology or innovation model used
  • Scalability potential
  • Social or economic impact
  • Sustainability and implementation strategy

Who Should Apply?

The challenge is ideal for:

  • Young entrepreneurs
  • Startup founders
  • Student innovators
  • Tech innovators
  • Social entrepreneurs
  • Sustainability innovators
  • Community change-makers
  • Youth-led startups and teams

Applicants with scalable and impact-driven ideas capable of addressing Africa’s pressing challenges are strongly encouraged to participate.

Deadline

At the time of publication, no official application deadline has been publicly announced. Interested applicants are encouraged to prepare and submit their applications as early as possible ahead of the October 2026 summit.

Final Thoughts

The Africa Youth Future Forward Innovation Challenge 2026 represents more than a competition. It is a strategic platform for empowering Africa’s next generation of innovators with the resources, exposure, and partnerships necessary to scale meaningful solutions.

For young African founders working on transformative ideas, this challenge offers a rare opportunity to access funding, mentorship, investor networks, and continental visibility while contributing to Africa’s future development.

As Africa continues to position itself as a global innovation hub, platforms like this play a critical role in unlocking the potential of youth-driven entrepreneurship and technology-led impact.

For more information click here 

Discover more opportunities here 

The post Africa Youth Future Forward Innovation Challenge 2026: Win Funding, Meet Investors, and Scale Your Impact Across Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Evidence Action Deputy Country Director Nigeria 2026: Fully Detailed Guide for International Development and Public Health Professionals
item.dc:creator: Ghai A. Deng
item.description:
Evidence Action has officially opened applications for the highly strategic and impactful position of Deputy Country Director in Nigeria for 2026. This senior leadership opportunity is designed for experienced professionals in international development, public health, program management, and government stakeholder engagement who are passionate about driving large-scale social impact across Africa. The position is based […]

The post Evidence Action Deputy Country Director Nigeria 2026: Fully Detailed Guide for International Development and Public Health Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Evidence Action has officially opened applications for the highly strategic and impactful position of Deputy Country Director in Nigeria for 2026. This senior leadership opportunity is designed for experienced professionals in international development, public health, program management, and government stakeholder engagement who are passionate about driving large-scale social impact across Africa.

The position is based in Abuja, Nigeria, and offers candidates the opportunity to work with one of the world’s leading evidence-driven global health organizations. The selected candidate will help lead and scale transformative health and development interventions that have already impacted hundreds of millions of people globally.

Professionals with strong leadership experience in multi-state program implementation, donor-funded initiatives, public health systems, and operational management are strongly encouraged to apply.

About Evidence Action

Evidence Action is a globally recognized nonprofit organization focused on delivering data-driven interventions that improve and save lives at massive scale. The organization specializes in identifying neglected global health issues and implementing solutions backed by rigorous academic evidence and operational efficiency.

The organization works closely with governments, partners, and local communities to ensure sustainable impact across multiple countries.

Currently operating in 11 countries with more than 900 staff members worldwide, Evidence Action has reached over 500 million people through innovative and evidence-based programs.

Evidence Action’s Global Impact

Evidence Action has built a strong reputation through several globally recognized interventions and programs.

Deworm the World Program

One of the organization’s flagship initiatives, Deworm the World, has delivered more than:

  • 2 billion deworming treatments globally

The program has significantly:

  • Reduced worm prevalence
  • Improved child health outcomes
  • Increased school participation
  • Generated over $23 billion in estimated lifetime productivity gains

Safe Water Now Initiative

Through its Safe Water Now initiative, Evidence Action has:

  • Saved the lives of more than 15,000 children

The program focuses on improving access to safe and clean drinking water in vulnerable communities.

Accelerator Program

Evidence Action’s Accelerator identifies and tests:

  • Low-cost interventions
  • High-impact health innovations
  • Scalable public health solutions

The goal is to explore untapped opportunities capable of dramatically improving health outcomes in underserved populations.

Deputy Country Director Nigeria 2026 Position Overview

Job Title

Deputy Country Director (Nigeria)

Organization

Evidence Action

Job Type

Full-Time

Work Arrangement

On-Site

Location

Abuja, Federal Capital Territory, Nigeria

Role Summary

The Deputy Country Director will serve as a senior member of Evidence Action’s Nigeria leadership team and will support the Country Director in overseeing the organization’s multi-state portfolio across Nigeria.

The successful candidate will:

  • Lead strategic implementation efforts
  • Strengthen operational systems
  • Support program execution
  • Coordinate stakeholder relationships
  • Enhance organizational performance
  • Manage teams and partnerships
  • Drive sustainable impact across Evidence Action programs

The Deputy Country Director will also deputize for the Country Director during periods of absence and transition.

This role requires a highly strategic, people-centered, and operationally strong professional capable of translating organizational goals into measurable results.

Reporting Structure

The Deputy Country Director will directly supervise or coordinate with:

  • Zonal Managers
  • Policy and Government Relations Senior Associate
  • Nutrition Program Manager
  • Nutrition Associates
  • Associate Manager MLE-D (dotted line)

Core Responsibilities

The role combines strategic leadership, operational oversight, stakeholder engagement, financial management, and team development.

1. Strategy and Operations

The Deputy Country Director will help shape and execute Evidence Action’s strategy in Nigeria.

Key Duties Include:

  • Supporting development of country strategies and annual plans
  • Translating strategic goals into operational activities
  • Managing implementation timelines and deliverables
  • Resolving operational and programmatic risks
  • Ensuring data-driven decision-making
  • Supporting organizational compliance requirements
  • Leading country operations in the absence of the Country Director

The role requires strong systems thinking and strategic execution capabilities.

2. Program Performance Management

The selected candidate will oversee implementation quality across multiple active states in Nigeria.

Responsibilities Include:

  • Monitoring service delivery standards
  • Reviewing monitoring and evaluation data
  • Identifying performance gaps
  • Implementing corrective actions
  • Coordinating with national and sub-national government stakeholders
  • Supporting evidence-based programming
  • Conducting field visits for implementation oversight
  • Supporting pilot interventions and new program integration

The candidate will ensure all interventions align with Evidence Action’s evidence-based operational model.

3. Team Leadership and Organizational Culture

Evidence Action is seeking a leader capable of building high-performing and mission-driven teams.

Team Leadership Duties:

  • Mentoring and supervising country team members
  • Promoting accountability and empowerment
  • Supporting recruitment and onboarding processes
  • Encouraging knowledge sharing and continuous learning
  • Embedding organizational values into team culture
  • Developing workforce capacity and performance

The ideal candidate should possess excellent interpersonal and people management skills.

4. Resource and Financial Management

The Deputy Country Director will support financial planning and donor accountability efforts.

Key Financial Responsibilities:

  • Supporting annual budgeting processes
  • Conducting budget-versus-actual analyses
  • Monitoring financial controls and compliance
  • Supporting donor reporting requirements
  • Encouraging cost-conscious program management
  • Assisting with funding proposals and due diligence documentation

Experience managing donor-funded programs is considered highly valuable for this role.

5. Government and Stakeholder Engagement

Government collaboration is central to Evidence Action’s operational approach.

The Successful Candidate Will:

  • Represent Evidence Action at government forums
  • Engage with ministries of health and stakeholders
  • Maintain strategic partnerships
  • Coordinate with civil society organizations
  • Support advocacy and partnership strategies
  • Identify potential program implementation partners

Strong government relations experience is essential.

6. New Program Development

The Deputy Country Director will also contribute to innovation and expansion initiatives.

Responsibilities Include:

  • Supporting pilot interventions
  • Conducting landscape analyses
  • Exploring high-potential interventions
  • Assisting in proposal development
  • Engaging prospective donors
  • Supporting program scale-up efforts

This component of the role is particularly suited for professionals passionate about innovation in public health and development.

Key Competencies Required

Evidence Action is seeking candidates with strong technical, strategic, and leadership competencies.

Core Competencies Include:

  • Strategic thinking
  • Operational execution
  • Team leadership
  • Program performance management
  • Budget and financial oversight
  • Stakeholder engagement
  • Donor relations
  • Problem-solving
  • Risk management
  • Cross-cultural communication
  • Collaboration and teamwork

Minimum Qualifications and Experience

Applicants must meet several important academic and professional requirements.

Educational Requirements

Candidates must possess:

  • An advanced degree in:
    • Public Health
    • Health Management
    • Social Sciences
    • Development Studies
    • Or related fields

Professional Experience Requirements

Applicants should have:

  • At least 7 years of progressively responsible program management experience
  • Minimum of 3 years in a senior leadership role
  • Experience managing multi-site programs in Nigeria or Sub-Saharan Africa
  • Strong team leadership experience
  • Experience using data for program decisions
  • Experience managing donor-funded projects

Preferred Experience

Candidates with the following experience will have an added advantage:

  • Public health programming
  • Nutrition programs
  • WASH initiatives
  • Neglected tropical disease programming
  • Malaria interventions
  • USAID-funded programs
  • Gates Foundation projects
  • GiveWell-funded initiatives
  • FCDO-supported projects

Language Requirements

Applicants must:

  • Be fluent in English

Additional advantage for:

  • Hausa
  • Yoruba
  • Other Nigerian languages

Employee Benefits

Evidence Action offers a competitive benefits package to support employee wellbeing and professional development.

Benefits Include:

  • Private Health Insurance
  • Paid Time Off
  • Training and Development Opportunities

The organization also promotes flexibility, teamwork, innovation, and purpose-driven work.

Why This Opportunity Is Important

The Deputy Country Director role offers a unique opportunity to contribute to transformative health and development initiatives across Nigeria.

Professionals selected for this role will:

  • Influence large-scale public health systems
  • Work directly with governments
  • Lead high-impact programs
  • Drive evidence-based interventions
  • Improve lives across vulnerable communities

This is an ideal opportunity for experienced development professionals seeking leadership roles with measurable social impact.

How to Apply

Interested candidates should apply through Evidence Action’s official recruitment platform.

Applicants are encouraged to prepare:

  • An updated CV or Resume
  • Relevant professional experience details
  • Leadership and program management achievements
  • Evidence of donor-funded project experience

Apply HERE

Equal Opportunity Statement

Evidence Action is committed to diversity, equity, and inclusion.

The organization does not discriminate based on:

  • Race
  • Gender
  • Religion
  • Disability
  • Sexual orientation
  • National origin
  • Veteran status
  • Age
  • Or other legally protected characteristics

 

For more information , visit the official website

Discover more job opportunities on OFY, VISIT

The post Evidence Action Deputy Country Director Nigeria 2026: Fully Detailed Guide for International Development and Public Health Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: ASU and Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program Announce Remote Africa-Based Coordinator Position
item.dc:creator: Ghai A. Deng
item.description:
The Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program in partnership with Arizona State University (ASU) has officially opened applications for an exciting full-time remote Coordinator position designed for African professionals who are alumni of the Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program. This opportunity is specifically aimed at supporting Mastercard Foundation Scholars enrolled in ASU’s online graduate programs at Kepler College […]

The post ASU and Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program Announce Remote Africa-Based Coordinator Position appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program in partnership with Arizona State University (ASU) has officially opened applications for an exciting full-time remote Coordinator position designed for African professionals who are alumni of the Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program.

This opportunity is specifically aimed at supporting Mastercard Foundation Scholars enrolled in ASU’s online graduate programs at Kepler College in Kigali, Rwanda. The selected coordinator will play a major role in academic support, virtual engagement, leadership development, and community-building for scholars from Sub-Saharan Africa.

Professionals passionate about education, youth development, online learning, student engagement, and social impact are strongly encouraged to apply before the application deadline of May 25, 2026, at 12:00 PM GMT.

About the Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program at ASU

The Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program at Arizona State University is committed to empowering young African leaders through accessible and innovative higher education opportunities. Through its collaboration with Kepler College in Kigali, Rwanda, ASU delivers online graduate education that supports leadership development, entrepreneurship, service, and community transformation.

The newly announced Coordinator role is designed to strengthen scholar engagement and improve the online student experience for Mastercard Foundation Scholars pursuing graduate studies remotely.

The position offers candidates the opportunity to contribute directly to educational transformation across Africa while working within a global and mission-driven team.

Position Overview

Job Title:

Coordinator – Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program at ASU (Africa-Based)

Organization

Arizona State University (ASU) in partnership with the Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program

Employment Type

Full-Time

Work Location

Remote (Applicants must be based in Africa)

Salary Range

36,000,000 RWF to 43,000,000 RWF

Main Purpose of the Role

Under the administrative direction of the Program Manager, the Coordinator will support Mastercard Foundation Scholars enrolled in ASU’s online graduate programs.

The role focuses heavily on:

  • Virtual advising
  • Student support services
  • Online engagement strategies
  • Leadership development
  • Community-building initiatives
  • Academic success support
  • Program coordination and logistics

The successful candidate will also serve as a key contact person for online scholars and help improve the delivery and assessment of virtual learning initiatives.

Key Responsibilities and Essential Duties

The Coordinator will perform a broad range of responsibilities to ensure scholars receive effective academic and community support throughout their programs.

Core Duties Include:

1. Program Planning and Coordination

  • Plan and facilitate major scholar activities and signature programs.
  • Coordinate orientation programs, monthly scholar meetings, and immersive summer experiences.
  • Work collaboratively with the Program Manager and broader Scholars Program team.

2. Student Advising and Academic Support

  • Serve as the primary contact person for online graduate scholars.
  • Monitor scholar academic performance and engagement.
  • Coordinate interventions and support systems with faculty, staff, and academic departments.

3. Virtual Engagement and Community Building

  • Lead reflective discussions and virtual engagement activities.
  • Promote critical thinking and community development among scholars.
  • Foster meaningful online learning experiences and peer connections.

4. Program Logistics and Operations

  • Coordinate logistics for both virtual and in-person program components.
  • Manage communication between scholars, faculty, staff, and external partners.
  • Support the smooth delivery of scholar services and activities.

5. Student Leadership and Social Innovation Support

  • Increase scholar awareness and participation in:
    • Volunteer opportunities
    • Service initiatives
    • Entrepreneurship programs
    • Social innovation activities

6. Administrative and Reporting Responsibilities

  • Maintain accurate student records and program data.
  • Prepare reports and assist with budget-related tracking.
  • Ensure program objectives align with timelines and funding guidelines.

7. Additional Assigned Duties

  • Carry out other responsibilities as assigned by the program leadership.

Minimum Qualifications Required

Applicants must meet all the following requirements to be considered for the role:

Educational Qualification

Candidates must possess a Master’s degree in one of the following or related fields:

  • Education
  • Public Administration
  • Social Work
  • Instructional Design
  • Global Development
  • Related disciplines

Mastercard Foundation Scholars Alumni Requirement

Applicants must be alumni of the Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program and should have completed the scholarship at either:

  • Undergraduate level, or
  • Graduate level

Additional Mandatory Requirements

Applicants must:

  • Demonstrate the ability to work independently with minimal supervision.
  • Be currently residing within the African continent.

Preferred Qualifications and Skills

While not mandatory, the following qualifications and experiences will strengthen an application:

  • Experience in online learning design and virtual facilitation
  • Knowledge of instructional content creation
  • Scholarship program coordination experience
  • Familiarity with Sub-Saharan African youth initiatives
  • Strong decision-making and stakeholder management skills
  • Experience supporting online classrooms or digital student engagement
  • Excellent communication and collaboration abilities

Work Environment and Expectations

The Coordinator role is fully remote and requires a high level of professionalism, flexibility, and collaboration.

Applicants Should Expect:

  • A dynamic virtual work environment
  • Independent and team-based responsibilities
  • Occasional evening or early morning work hours to accommodate global time zones
  • Cross-functional collaboration with international teams
  • Regular communication with scholars, staff, and institutional partners

The role is ideal for professionals who thrive in mission-driven educational environments and are passionate about empowering African youth through higher education.

Salary and Benefits

The selected candidate will receive a competitive salary ranging between:

  • 36,000,000 RWF and 43,000,000 RWF

Beyond financial compensation, the opportunity also offers:

  • International work exposure
  • Leadership development experience
  • Direct contribution to African education and youth empowerment
  • Collaboration with global academic professionals
  • Experience managing high-impact virtual learning initiatives

Required Application Materials

Interested candidates must submit the following documents:

  1. Updated Resume
  2. Cover Letter
  3. Three Professional References

How to Apply

Applicants should send their application materials via email to: keplerasuscholars@asu.edu

Email Subject Line

Africa Based Coordinator Position: First_Last Name

Application Deadline

The deadline to apply for this opportunity is: May 25, 2026, at 12:00 PM GMT

Applicants are strongly encouraged to submit their applications early to avoid missing the deadline.

Why This Opportunity Matters

This position represents more than just employment. It is an opportunity to directly impact the educational journeys of African scholars while helping shape future leaders across the continent.

By joining the Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program at ASU, the selected candidate will become part of a transformative initiative focused on:

  • Educational equity
  • Leadership development
  • Youth empowerment
  • Innovation
  • Community impact

For Mastercard Foundation Scholars alumni seeking meaningful, mission-driven careers in education and student development, this opportunity provides an ideal platform for growth and impact.

Fore more information about the opportunity, Click Here

Discover more job opportunities on OFY, VISIT

The post ASU and Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program Announce Remote Africa-Based Coordinator Position appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: OECD Internship Programme 2026: Fully Flexible International Internship Opportunity for Students Worldwide
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD) has officially opened applications for its highly competitive Internship Programme, offering students from around the world an opportunity to work alongside global policy experts and specialists in an international environment. This internship programme provides students with hands-on experience in policy development, research, data analysis, communication, administration, and […]

The post OECD Internship Programme 2026: Fully Flexible International Internship Opportunity for Students Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD) has officially opened applications for its highly competitive Internship Programme, offering students from around the world an opportunity to work alongside global policy experts and specialists in an international environment. This internship programme provides students with hands-on experience in policy development, research, data analysis, communication, administration, and international cooperation while contributing to the OECD’s mission of building better policies for better lives.

The OECD Internship Programme is designed for motivated and talented students who are currently enrolled in bachelor’s, master’s, or PhD programmes and wish to strengthen their analytical, technical, and professional skills within one of the world’s most influential international organisations.

Unlike many internship opportunities with strict deadlines, the OECD Internship Programme accepts applications on a rolling basis throughout the year, making it easier for eligible candidates to apply at a convenient time.

Applicants can work in-person in Paris, remotely, or in hybrid arrangements depending on the needs of the hiring team, making this one of the most flexible international internship opportunities currently available.

About the OECD Internship Programme

The OECD Internship Programme aims to bring diverse and curious students into the organisation to support its work across multiple policy sectors and corporate functions. Interns become part of a multicultural and international environment where they collaborate with economists, policy analysts, communication experts, researchers, and government specialists.

The organisation works closely with governments worldwide on major global issues including:

  • Artificial intelligence
  • Climate change
  • Education and skills
  • Economic development
  • International trade
  • Taxation
  • Digital transformation
  • Science and innovation
  • Employment
  • Governance
  • Environment
  • Health systems
  • Regional development
  • Finance and investment

Interns contribute directly to projects that influence policies affecting millions of people globally.

What Interns Will Do

Selected interns may be assigned responsibilities such as:

Research and Analysis

  • Conducting research on policy issues
  • Reviewing existing materials and reports
  • Collecting, organising, and analysing data
  • Supporting evidence-based policy recommendations

Documentation and Publications

  • Preparing studies and policy documents
  • Drafting notes and technical annexes
  • Assisting with publications and working papers
  • Supporting report development processes

Meetings and Networking

  • Participating in international meetings and seminars
  • Liaising with government officials and researchers
  • Building networks with international organisations
  • Collaborating with member country delegations

Event and Communication Support

  • Assisting with event planning and coordination
  • Supporting communication campaigns
  • Helping with planning and evaluation activities

Areas Where Interns May Work

Policy Areas

Interns may support policy analysts in departments such as:

  1. Agriculture and Fisheries
  2. Climate Change
  3. Development
  4. Digital Economy
  5. Education and Skills
  6. Employment
  7. Environment
  8. Finance and Investment
  9. Governance
  10. Health
  11. Industry, Business and Entrepreneurship
  12. Science, Technology and Innovation
  13. Society
  14. Taxation
  15. Trade
  16. Regional, Rural and Urban Development
  17. International Energy Agency
  18. Nuclear Energy Agency
  19. International Transport Forum

Corporate Functions

Students with administrative or operational backgrounds may also work in:

  • Audit
  • Communications and Public Relations
  • Event Management
  • Finance and Accounting
  • Human Resources
  • Information Technology
  • Legal Services
  • Office Administration
  • Translation Services

Internship Duration

The OECD Internship Programme runs for:

  • Between 1 and 6 months initially
  • Renewable up to 12 months total

Internships are generally:

  • Full-time (40 hours per week)
  • Occasionally part-time (minimum 20 hours weekly under exceptional circumstances)

The OECD strongly encourages students to pursue internships lasting at least four months to maximize the experience.

Internship Location and Work Flexibility

Most internships are based at the OECD headquarters in Paris, France. However, the programme offers flexible arrangements including:

  • In-person internships
  • Hybrid internships
  • Fully remote internships

The working arrangement depends on the hiring department’s needs.

Financial Benefits

Successful interns located in Paris receive financial support including:

Monthly Allowance

  • €1,000 per full month worked

Leave Benefits

  • 2.5 working days of leave per full month worked
  • French public holidays observed

These benefits help interns manage living expenses while gaining international work experience.

Eligibility Requirements

To qualify for the OECD Internship Programme, applicants must:

Academic Requirements

  • Be enrolled full-time in an accredited degree programme
  • Bachelor’s, Master’s, and PhD students are eligible
  • Remain enrolled throughout the internship duration

Skills and Competencies

Applicants should possess:

  • Strong analytical and research skills
  • Good quantitative and IT skills
  • Excellent drafting and communication abilities
  • Ability to work in multicultural teams

Language Requirements

Candidates must have:

  • Excellent command of either English or French
  • Working knowledge of the second official language or willingness to learn it

Knowledge of additional languages is considered an advantage.

Important Note on Accredited Programmes

The OECD does not recognise:

  • Non-degree online courses
  • Programmes without physical attendance requirements
  • Non-accredited academic programmes

Applicants must therefore ensure their institution and programme meet accreditation standards.

Selection Process

The internship recruitment process involves four stages:

1. Online Application

Candidates submit:

  • Online application form
  • Cover letter via the SmartRecruiters platform

2. Pre-Screening

Applications are reviewed by the Internship Office and shortlisted for directorates.

3. Interview or Written Test

Hiring teams may conduct:

  • Interviews
  • Written assessments

4. Internship Offer

Selected candidates receive:

  • Internship agreement
  • Official offer documentation

Rolling Applications and Recruitment Timeline

One major advantage of the OECD Internship Programme is its rolling application system.

Applications are accepted year-round, allowing students flexibility in planning their internship period.

However, candidates should apply well in advance because:

  • The recruitment process may take up to three months
  • Hiring depends on departmental needs and candidate availability

Applicants can also update their availability after submission through their application portal.

Why You Should Apply for the OECD Internship Programme

The OECD Internship Programme offers numerous benefits including:

International Exposure

Work within a globally recognised international organisation.

Professional Networking

Connect with policymakers, researchers, economists, and international experts.

Career Development

Build technical, analytical, and communication skills highly valued worldwide.

Flexible Work Options

Choose between remote, hybrid, or in-person work arrangements depending on availability.

Real Policy Impact

Contribute to policy solutions addressing global challenges.

APPLY HERE

Deadline

There is no fixed deadline. Applications are accepted on a rolling basis throughout the year.

Final Thoughts

The OECD Internship Programme remains one of the most prestigious international internship opportunities for students seeking global exposure, policy experience, and professional growth. Whether your interests lie in economics, climate change, governance, technology, communications, finance, or international development, the programme provides a unique platform to gain practical experience while contributing to meaningful global initiatives.

Students from diverse academic and cultural backgrounds are encouraged to apply and become part of an organisation committed to shaping better policies for better lives.

For more information about this opportunity, visit here.

Discover more Global Internships Opportunities on OFY here.

The post OECD Internship Programme 2026: Fully Flexible International Internship Opportunity for Students Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: YES-MED Call for Participants 2026: International Youth Empowerment Programme for Mediterranean Youth in Social and Solidarity Economy
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
Young people across the Mediterranean region now have the opportunity to participate in the transformative YES-MED Project 2026, an international initiative focused on youth empowerment, employability, entrepreneurship, social innovation, and sustainable development. The project, officially titled Youth Employment in Solidarity – Mixing Experiences and Diversity (YES-MED), aims to strengthen the socio-economic integration of young people […]

The post YES-MED Call for Participants 2026: International Youth Empowerment Programme for Mediterranean Youth in Social and Solidarity Economy appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Young people across the Mediterranean region now have the opportunity to participate in the transformative YES-MED Project 2026, an international initiative focused on youth empowerment, employability, entrepreneurship, social innovation, and sustainable development. The project, officially titled Youth Employment in Solidarity – Mixing Experiences and Diversity (YES-MED), aims to strengthen the socio-economic integration of young people by equipping them with practical skills, international exposure, and opportunities within the Social and Solidarity Economy (SSE) sector.

The YES-MED project is designed for young individuals aged 18 to 35 from six Mediterranean countries and offers an engaging learning journey that combines training, networking, entrepreneurship, international cooperation, sustainability, and youth-led action.

With a focus on creating socially responsible employment opportunities and fostering sustainable community development, YES-MED will engage 120 young participants in a dynamic training and mobility programme between September and October 2026.

What is the YES-MED Project?

The YES-MED Project is an international youth empowerment initiative implemented by seven partner organizations across the Mediterranean region. The project seeks to create stronger pathways for youth employment and active participation in society through Social and Solidarity Economy (SSE) models and international cooperation.

The initiative recognizes the growing need for sustainable employment opportunities for youth while also addressing social inclusion, environmental responsibility, entrepreneurship, and community resilience.

Through a comprehensive training pathway and mobility opportunities, YES-MED aims to empower young people to become active changemakers capable of designing and implementing impactful initiatives in their local communities and beyond.

Participating Countries

The YES-MED project is open to young people from the following Mediterranean countries:

  • Italy
  • France
  • Spain
  • Tunisia
  • Morocco
  • Lebanon

Participants from these countries will collaborate in an intercultural and international learning environment that promotes diversity, inclusion, cooperation, and shared experiences.

Main Objective of the YES-MED Programme

The overall goal of the YES-MED initiative is to strengthen the socio-economic integration of young people aged 18–35 by:

  • Enhancing employability skills
  • Promoting entrepreneurship
  • Encouraging sustainable and eco-conscious practices
  • Supporting youth participation in Social and Solidarity Economy initiatives
  • Building international cooperation networks
  • Empowering youth to lead community-based projects
  • Encouraging socially responsible innovation

The programme seeks to create long-term positive impact by enabling participants to transfer their knowledge and skills into practical actions within their communities.

The Youth Mediterranean Empowerment Path (WP3)

One of the core components of the YES-MED project is the Youth Mediterranean Empowerment Path, an international learning journey specifically designed for young multipliers.

A total of 120 young participants will be selected to join this intensive training programme.

Duration of the Training

Participants will complete approximately:

  • 40 hours of training
  • Between September and October 2026

The programme is jointly designed by project partners and tailored to participants’ interests, backgrounds, and local realities.

What Participants Will Learn

The YES-MED training programme combines theoretical knowledge with practical learning approaches. Participants will gain valuable competencies in areas that are increasingly important for sustainable development and future employment.

Key Training Areas Include:

1. Social and Solidarity Economy (SSE)

Participants will explore alternative economic models focused on:

  • Social impact
  • Community development
  • Inclusion
  • Sustainability
  • Ethical entrepreneurship

They will learn how SSE initiatives can help address social and economic challenges within Mediterranean communities.

2. International Cooperation

The programme introduces participants to the principles and practices of international collaboration, including:

  • Partnership building
  • Cross-cultural cooperation
  • Community engagement
  • International development perspectives

3. Digital Skills Development

Participants will improve essential digital competencies that can support:

  • Entrepreneurship
  • Communication
